LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF KWIK BOND POLYMERS, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE DENIED. KWIK BOND HAS NOT SATISFIED ITS INITIAL SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN. BECAUSE THERE IS NO MANDATORY DUTY AS ALLEGED IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, CALTRANS IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT IN ITS FAVOR. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING VERBATIM AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. (SEE MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Off Calendar Nov-09-2016 Set For Case Management Conference On Feb-08-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

Defendant California Department of Transportation's motion for summary judgment is granted. This motion was argued on October 13, 2016 and the hearing was continued to October 25, 2016 for the sole purpose of issuance of the court's ruling. After review of the additional papers filed by the parties concerning the legislative history of Public Contract Code 10225, the court believes that section 10225 does not impose any mandatory duty on CalTrans after a payment bond has been filed that complies with the requirements for such a bond. Accordingly, the court now adopts the tentative ruling issued in advance of the October 13 hearing: Notwithstanding the contrary ruling by Judge Goldsmith on Caltrans' demurrer to the second amended complaint, there is no mandatory duty imposed by any of the statutes cited in the second amended complaint or in Kwik Bonds' papers on this motion that requires Caltrans to ensure the efficacy of a payment bond after the payment bond once a payment bond has been filed that complies with the requirements for such a bond. The undisputed facts establish that Caltrans ensured that a statutorily compliant payment bond was filed as to each of the public work contracts at issue in this case prior to the execution of the contract for each of the public work projects. Having done so, Caltrans discharged the sole mandatory duty imposed on it with regard to the securing of a payment bond as to each public work project. There is no California authority that imposed any further duty on Caltrans such as a duty to ensure the efficacy of the payment bond in light of the financial, legal or other circumstances of the general contractor who filed the bond or the surety who issued the bond. Public Contract Code 10225 does not impose such a duty and, even if it did, that duty would be limited to circumstances that do not obtain here where the surety cancelled or withdrew a payment bond. Because there is no mandatory duty as alleged in the second amended complaint, Caltrans is entitled to judgment in its favor. Counsel for Caltrans is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim all portions of this ruling except for this sentence and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication; Notice Of Motion
Off Calendar Oct-25-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

In re: PLAINTIFF KWIK BOND POLYMERS, LLC, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication - This motion was argued on October 13, 2016 and the hearing was continued to October 25, 2016 for the sole purpose of issuance of the court's ruling. After review of the additional papers filed by the parties concerning the legislative history of Public Contract Code 10225, the court believes that section 10225 does not impose any mandatory duty on CalTrans after a payment bond has been filed that complies with the requirements for such a bond. Accordingly, the court now adopts the tentative ruling issued in advance of the October 13 hearing: Plaintiff Kwik Bond Polymers, LLC's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Kwik Bond has not satisfied its initial summary judgment burden. Kwik Bond cannot show Caltrans breached any mandatory duty imposed by statute. Kwik Bond does not cite any authority imposing a mandatory duty requiring Caltrans to halt payments to general contractor Victory Engineers, Inc. or to obtain a replacement bond in the event that the surety was in liquidation proceedings. Notwithstanding the contrary ruling by Judge Goldsmith on Caltrans' demurrer to the second amended complaint, there is no mandatory duty imposed by any of the statutes cited in the second amended complaint or in Kwik Bonds' papers on this motion that requires Caltrans to ensure the efficacy of a payment bond after the payment bond once a payment bond has been filed that complies with the requirements for such a bond. The undisputed facts establish that Caltrans ensured that a statutorily compliant payment bond was filed as to each of the public work contracts at issue in this case prior to the execution of the contract for each of the public work projects. Having done so, Caltrans discharged the sole mandatory duty imposed on it with regard to the securing of a payment bond as to each public work project. There is no California authority that imposed any further duty on Caltrans such as a duty to ensure the efficacy of the payment bond in light of the financial, legal or other circumstances of the general contractor who filed the bond or the surety who issued the bond. Public Contract Code 10225 does not impose such a duty and, even if it did, that duty would be limited to circumstances that do not obtain here where the surety cancelled or withdrew a payment bond. Because there is no mandatory duty as alleged in the second amended complaint, Caltrans is entitled to judgment in its favor. The prevailing party is to prepare a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-04-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ALLEN W. ESTES III OF GORDON & REES LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-986-5900 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ANKUSH AGARWAL, DEPUTY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 1-510-433-9100 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. CIVIL CODE SECTIONS 3247 AND CIVIL CODE SECTIONS 3248 ESTABLISH THAT CALTRANS HAS A STATUTORY DUTY TO ENSURE THAT THE PAYMENT BOND FOR THE PROJECT WERE GOOD AND SUFFICIENT. THE LEGISLATURE REPEALED SECTIONS 3247 AND 3248 IN 2010 BUT DID NOT IMPLEMENT THE REPEALS UNTIL JULY 2012. DUE TO THE EFFECTIVENESS OF SECTION 3247 AT THE TIME THE PARTIES CONTRACTED, KWIK BOND'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES STATE A SUFFICIENT CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MANDATORY DUTY. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-09-2015 Continued To May-27-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-04-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CALTRANS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Defendant is a public entity and therefore any claims against it must be based upon statute imposing liability. Plaintiff has not alleged any statutory basis for its claims for quantum meruit, negligence, or negligence per se. Plaintiff has leave to amend to allege, if it can in good faith, a statutory basis for liability against Defendant. Moreover, Civil Code section 9550 explicitly states that it does not apply to state entities, and while not pled directly in the First Amended Complaint, the Court takes judicial notice of the fact that, as a department of the State of California, Defendant is a state entity and therefore section 9550 does not apply here. (CC 9550(e).) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 =302/ehg
